hook: Cellar smørrebrød institution serving open sandwiches and snaps since the 19th century.
facts: Traditional Danish lunch restaurant on Hauser Plads 16, in the cellar, in central Copenhagen (Indre By).
Has served freshly made smørrebrød since 1877, making it one of Copenhagen's oldest eateries.
Known for an enormous selection of snaps (akvavit) to accompany the open sandwiches.
House classics include matured pickled herring, breaded plaice fillet, smoked eel with scrambled eggs and beef tartare.
Located at Hauser Plads 16, 1127 København K.

sources.note: Official site: open sandwiches since 1877; menu classics.
VisitCopenhagen: traditional lunch restaurant, smørrebrød + snaps, cellar location.
OSM Nominatim coords for Hauser Plads 16: lat 55.68277, lon 12.57608.

w: A cellar of smørrebrød on Hauser Plads since 1877, and a Copenhagen lunch institution — pickled herring, breaded plaice, smoked eel with scrambled egg, washed down from a snaps list as long as your arm. One of the city's oldest restaurants, and still the benchmark for the open sandwich. Book ahead; the locals do.

hook: Legendary jazz club that was the European home of expat American jazz giants in the 1960s.
facts: Opened in 1959 by Anders Dyrup; moved to Store Regnegade in 1961, where it made its name.
From 1959 to 1976 it was a European base for Dexter Gordon, Ben Webster, Stan Getz, Kenny Drew and other jazz masters.
Closed in 1995 after losing its identity, then reopened in May 2010 at its original Store Regnegade address.
Located at Store Regnegade 19A, 1110 København K.
Iconic masks created by Danish artist Mogens Gylling became a symbol of the club in the 1960s-70s.
legend: Patrons reportedly sometimes crept in under the piano to hear the music more clearly in the candlelit, smoke-filled room.

sources.note: Wikipedia: founding 1959, history, relocations, coords 55.6815 / 12.5817.
Official site: history as European home for 1960s-70s jazz giants; 2010 reopening.

w: For two decades from 1959, this was where America's exiled jazz giants came to play — Dexter Gordon, Ben Webster, Stan Getz and Kenny Drew made a European home of a candlelit, smoke-filled Copenhagen room. It went quiet in 1995 and reopened at the original address in 2010. Regulars used to crawl under the piano to hear better.

hook: Waterfront natural-wine bar run by importers Rosforth & Rosforth, a Noma-crowd hangout.
facts: Natural / low-intervention wine bar at Havnegade 53A, on the harbourfront near Nyhavn looking across to Christianshavn.
Operated by wine merchants Rosforth & Rosforth, who supply many of Copenhagen's top restaurants.
Celebrated ten years (opened ca. 2014) as a leader of Copenhagen's low-intervention wine scene.
Pours an extensive selection of natural wines by the glass, with a Georgian khachapuri food angle.
A known hangout for Noma staff, including chef René Redzepi.

sources.note: VisitCopenhagen: Rosforth & Rosforth-run natural wine bar at Havnegade 53A.
OSM Nominatim coords for Havnegade 53A: lat 55.67868, lon 12.59242.

w: A natural-wine bar on the harbour's edge near Nyhavn, run by the Rosforth importers who stock half of Copenhagen's best kitchens — so the Noma crowd, René Redzepi included, drift in. Low-intervention bottles by the glass, Georgian khachapuri to soak it up, the water right outside. Raw wine with a harbour view.

hook: The roastery-café that brought specialty coffee to Copenhagen, founded by a World Barista Champion.
facts: The Coffee Collective began as a roastery in 2007 and opened its first café on Jægersborggade, Nørrebro, in 2008.
The Jægersborggade premises are the oldest in their history, where they installed a large roaster.
Co-founded by Klaus Thomsen, the 2006 World Barista Champion, a pioneer of Scandinavian specialty coffee.
Known for lighter roasts, direct-trade sourcing and a transparent, acidity-forward Nordic style.
Located at Jægersborggade 57, 2200 København N.

sources.note: Official: roastery 2007, Jægersborggade café 2008, oldest location.
Wonderful Copenhagen: Klaus Thomsen, World Barista Champion, coffee pioneer.
OSM Nominatim coords for Jægersborggade 57: lat 55.69366, lon 12.54240.

w: The roastery that taught Copenhagen to take coffee seriously — founded in 2007 by Klaus Thomsen, a World Barista Champion, with its first café opening on Jægersborggade the year after. Light Nordic roasts, direct trade, acidity up front: the template the whole city followed. The original site, still roasting.

hook: Underground-music record shop and institution on Nørrebro's Blågårdsgade.
facts: Independent record shop at Blågårdsgade 31, 2200 København N, in Nørrebro.
Regarded as something of an institution on Copenhagen's underground music scene.
Carries a wide selection of both used and new vinyl as well as cassettes.
Sits on Blågårdsgade, one of Nørrebro's most characterful, café-lined pedestrian streets.

sources.note: Insula Music listing: Blågårdsgade 31; underground-music institution; used + new vinyl/cassettes.
OSM Nominatim coords for Blågårdsgade 31: lat 55.68580, lon 12.55763.

w: An underground-music record shop on Blågårdsgade, one of Nørrebro's most characterful café-lined streets — new and used vinyl, cassettes too, and a quiet reputation as an institution of Copenhagen's deeper music scene. Small, sharp, and well dug-in. Flip the crates and find something.

hook: Romantic royal landscape garden with canals where you can glimpse the zoo's elephants.
facts: Romantic English-style landscape garden of around 32 hectares at the western edge of inner Copenhagen, beneath Frederiksberg Palace.
Founded by King Frederik IV in the 1690s as the garden of his summer palace; redesigned in the romantic style in the 1790s.
From 1804 King Frederik VI sailed the garden's canals in a gondola, a feature he was especially attached to.
Together with adjacent Søndermarken it forms a 64-hectare green area.
Visitors can watch the elephants of the neighbouring Copenhagen Zoo across a low fence, after Norman Foster's Elephant House replaced the old dividing wall.

sources.note: Wikipedia: founding by Frederik IV 1690s, romantic redesign, Frederik VI's gondola, coords ~55.675 / 12.525.
Visit Copenhagen: elephants visible over the fence from the gardens; romantic park description.

w: A romantic royal garden of canals and lawns below Frederiksberg Palace, laid out for Frederik IV in the 1690s — his successor Frederik VI liked to be gondolaed along the waterways. Its quietest pleasure now is free: stand at the low western fence and watch the zoo's elephants, nothing but air between you. The city's loveliest stroll.

hook: Tree-lined cemetery-park where H.C. Andersen and Kierkegaard lie, and locals picnic.
facts: Inaugurated in 1760 as a burial ground for the poor, to relieve crowded graveyards inside the city walls.
Resting place of Hans Christian Andersen, Søren Kierkegaard, Niels Bohr, H.C. Ørsted and painters Købke and Eckersberg.
Also holds expat American jazz musicians who settled in Copenhagen, including Ben Webster and Kenny Drew.
Doubles as a public park in the Romantic style with rare old trees, used by locals for picnics and strolls.
Located in Nørrebro, Copenhagen.

sources.note: Wikipedia: founded 1760, Nørrebro; graves of Andersen, Kierkegaard, Bohr, Ørsted; coords 55.69111 / 12.54944.
Danish Architecture Center: Kierkegaard and H.C. Andersen buried here; cemetery-as-park.

w: A Nørrebro graveyard that doubles as the neighbourhood's park — Copenhageners picnic and stroll among old trees, between the graves of Hans Christian Andersen and Søren Kierkegaard. Opened in 1760 for the city's poor, it now holds Niels Bohr, Ørsted, and the exiled jazzmen Ben Webster and Kenny Drew. The dead in very good company.

n: Vor Frelsers Kirke (Church of Our Saviour)
hook: Baroque church whose external corkscrew spire you climb for one of the city's best views.
facts: Baroque church in Christianshavn at Sankt Annæ Gade 29, famous for its external spiral staircase spire.
The 90-metre spire, designed by Lauritz de Thurah and completed in 1752, winds counterclockwise four times to the top.
Around 400 steps lead to a viewing platform with panoramic views over the canals and rooftops.
The counterclockwise spiral was intentional, inspired by the lantern of Sant'Ivo alla Sapienza in Rome.
legend: A persistent myth says the architect leapt to his death on realising the spire spiralled the 'wrong' way; in fact de Thurah died in bed seven years later with no sign of dissatisfaction.

sources.note: Wikipedia: spire history, 1752, counterclockwise spiral, architect-suicide myth debunked, coords 55.67278 / 12.59389.
History Hit: external spiral staircase, climb for views over central Copenhagen.

w: A baroque church in Christianshavn with a spire you climb on the outside — a black-and-gold corkscrew winding counterclockwise to the top, 400-odd steps up over the canals and rooftops. De Thurah finished it in 1752, the reverse twist deliberate. Legend says he jumped when he saw it spiralled wrong; he didn't, dying in bed years later.

hook: A drained Victorian drinking-water reservoir under Søndermarken, now a dripping cathedral-like art space.
facts: Former underground drinking-water reservoir beneath Søndermarken park, across from Frederiksberg Palace.
Excavated from 1856 over about three years; could hold up to 16 million litres of water.
Built in response to Copenhagen's cholera outbreaks and need for clean water; served until 1933 and was drained in 1981.
Reopened in 1996, during Copenhagen's year as European City of Culture, as a contemporary-art space.
Part of Frederiksbergmuseerne since 2013; the cold, dark, moist halls evoke catacombs, with site-specific installations.

sources.note: Wikipedia: 1856 construction, 16 million litres, drained 1981, art space from 1996, coords 55.66953 / 12.52453.
VisitCopenhagen: former reservoir under Søndermarken, now contemporary-art exhibition space.

w: A Victorian reservoir drained of its 16 million litres, buried under the Søndermarken — dug in the 1850s after cholera to give Copenhagen clean water, emptied in 1981. Since 1996 the cold, dripping, cathedral-dark halls have held contemporary art, stalactites forming as you walk. Catacombs by accident, gallery by design.
